summary: I came to the United States in 2000, and for me, it is the best country with amazing opportunities for immigrants. Here, my children and I can improve our education, learn new languages, and enjoy a higher quality of life in a safe environment. The United States also offers excellent medical services, friendly people, beautiful nature, and vibrant cultural life. Although the first years were difficult, I have worked hard as a PCA and ALS specialist, and I am pursuing a nursing diploma to become a nurse practitioner. Living in the United States has allowed me to build a family and achieve my dreams.

Minnesota is the best state for family life despite its extreme climate. I appreciate its good infrastructure, less traffic, and a more relaxed pace of life that gives me enough time to rest. Living here makes me very happy because it balances work and personal life well. This state has helped me and my family settle comfortably after immigrating to the United States. It truly feels like home for us.

Russia is my home country, a big and strong nation where I graduated from the pediatrics academy in St. Petersburg and worked as a doctor. Medicine and education were free there, and I could speak my native languages and stay close to my friends, relatives, and culture. However, Russia faced many problems during my life, including political issues and the war in Afghanistan, which caused many deaths and social challenges. Despite having a good job and an apartment, I chose to immigrate to the United States for better opportunities and family life. Russia will always be part of my identity, but my future is here now.

I studied medicine and graduated from the pediatrics academy in Saint Petersburg, where I also lived in my own apartment. It was in Saint Petersburg that I met my husband, who is originally from Bulgaria but had lived in the United States for a long time. We connected quickly, and after a year of marriage, we have been together for fifteen years with two children. Saint Petersburg holds many memories for me, including my education, career, and the beginning of my family life. It was a significant place before I moved to the United States.

During my life in Russia, the war in Afghanistan was a major event that affected many people. It caused the loss of many men, which led to a social imbalance where women outnumber men and face competition for single men. This war was part of the political problems Russia experienced in the last century. The impact of the war influenced my perspective on life in Russia and contributed to my decision to seek a new life in the United States. It was a difficult time for my country and for many families.

My husband is originally from Bulgaria, though he has lived in the United States for a long time. We met in Saint Petersburg, where he was visiting and spoke Russian fluently, loving Russian culture. Despite his recent divorce and being very upset, he found comfort in Saint Petersburg and proposed to me after only three days. We married after a year and have built a life together here in the United States with two children and our dream house. Bulgaria is part of my family's story through him, connecting our diverse backgrounds.
